title: Markham, Nick - Parliamentary Under Secretary of State, Department for Health and Social Care - ACOBA advice
body: "The Lord Markham CBE left his role at the Department for Health and Social Care in July 2024.  Advisor, GHO Capital  The Lord Markham CBE sought the Committee’s advice about taking up an appointment with GHO Capital. The letter containing the Committee’s consideration and the conditions imposed on the appointment was sent in February 2025; and the appointment was taken up later that month. An accessible version of the letter is available here.  Advisor, Keles Advisory SAS  The Lord Markham CBE sought the Committee’s advice about taking up an appointment with Keles Advisory SAS. The letter containing the Committee’s consideration and the conditions imposed on the appointment was sent in February 2025; and the appointment was taken up later that month. An accessible version of the letter is available here.  President, the British Fluoridation Society  The Lord Markham CBE sought the Committee’s advice about taking up an appointment with the British Fluoridation Society. The letter containing the Committee’s consideration and the conditions imposed on the appointment was sent in December 2024; and the appointment was announced in January 2025. An accessible version of the letter is available here.  Non-Executive Director, Express Test Investment Ltd  The Lord Markham CBE sought the Committee’s advice about taking up an appointment with Express Test Investment Ltd. The letter containing the Committee’s consideration and the conditions imposed on the appointment was sent in November 2024; and the appointment was taken up in January 2025. An accessible version of the letter is available here.  Non-Executive Director, Pennys XI  The Lord Markham CBE sought the Committee’s advice about taking up an appointment with Pennys XI. The letter containing the Committee’s consideration and the conditions imposed on the appointment was sent in October 2024; and the appointment was taken up later that month. An accessible version of the letter is available here. "
